tracks.meaning: The highness or lowness of a sound, determined by the frequency of vibration of the sound waves produced by it.
A persuasive sales presentation or proposal made to potential investors, clients, or customers.
The act of throwing a ball toward a batter in baseball or softball.
A dark, sticky, viscous substance derived from tar, coal, or petroleum, used for waterproofing and binding.
The angle or degree of slope of a roof, hillside, or other inclined surface.
To throw or hurl something forcefully.
The act of falling forward or being thrown violently in a particular direction.
The forward and backward tilting motion of a ship moving through waves.
A resinous substance exuded by various plants, particularly pine trees.
The tone, modulation, or delivery used when speaking or presenting information.
A sales talk or advertising line delivered with the intent to persuade or convince someone.
The amount of money wagered or the stakes in a betting situation.
The distance between corresponding points on adjacent teeth of a gear or screw thread.
The distance between the top of one line of type and the top of the next line.
The angle that a layer of rock or mineral vein makes with the horizontal plane.
A temporary military camp or fortified position.
The perceived quality or characteristic manner of delivering lines or performing on stage.
